subtitle: Senior Data & Infrastructure Leader -- 75+ Org Scope | Production ML | Mission-Driven Practice
summary_text: Senior data and infrastructure leader with 14 years owning the full technology function at Money Management International -- a national 501(c)(3) nonprofit providing financial counseling, debt management, and consumer financial education. Built and led a 35+ direct (75+ extended) technology team across data, infrastructure, software engineering, and global vendor partners. Owned the data function end-to-end -- DBA / SQL Architect origin, enterprise data warehouse maturation, and production ML deployment via DataRobot for credit and servicing decisions in a regulated environment where model failures have real consequences for real people's financial lives. Currently consulting on AI architecture, automation, and implementation. Comfortable across the build-buy-partner spectrum at scale, with executive-level fluency in data quality, governance, and ML operations.

bullets: Built an end-to-end AI architecture evaluation framework for generative and agentic AI platforms -- scoring rubric, capability taxonomy, build-buy-partner decision model -- adopted by a national CX consulting firm.

Provide client-facing advisory on generative and agentic AI architectures, automation, and implementation; translate executive priorities into architectural trade-offs and concrete platform decisions.
Hands-on AI-assisted engineering daily with Claude and GitHub Copilot; published thinking on AI adoption realities, the automation-vs-AI distinction, and reflexive AI governance practice on LinkedIn.

Directed enterprise technology for a national 501(c)(3) nonprofit providing financial counseling, debt management, and consumer financial education -- under continuous regulatory scrutiny across all 50 states plus federal regulators including the CFPB, with HIPAA, PCI-DSS, and CARD Act overlays.

Built and led a 35+ direct / 75+ extended technology organization -- including dedicated data, infrastructure, software engineering, and global vendor partner teams; team-of-teams structure including Head of Data, Head of Infrastructure, ePMO leader, and two software development leads.

Owned the data function end-to-end for 14 years -- DBA / SQL Architect origin, enterprise data warehouse maturation (reactive reporting -> proper DW -> self-service trajectory), and production ML deployment via DataRobot for credit and servicing use cases in a regulated environment where model failures have real consequences.

Owned infrastructure as a real function: multi-generation end-user compute transitions, physical datacenter to cloud/hybrid migration, evolution of the security landscape, server and desktop patching, security scanning and response. Not hands-on -- led, prioritized, and understood the function with depth.

Led build-buy-partner strategy across the enterprise: contact center platform transformation (intelligent routing, AI-assisted coaching, integrated knowledge bots, zero service disruption on day-one go-live); multi-year vendor relationships across infrastructure managed services, offshore development partners, and specialized consultancies.

Owned multi-year platform roadmap for MMI's homegrown ERP/CRM for 8 years across 12+ executive and operations stakeholders; multi-factor prioritization model (ROI x user satisfaction x efficiency x cross-departmental equity) governed sprint-over-sprint decisions across a 45-50 person delivery organization.
Sustained 100% PCI-DSS certification across multiple audit cycles alongside HIPAA and CARD Act compliance; sustained highest retention and satisfaction in the enterprise across 14 years.
Advanced through progressive roles: DBA / SQL Architect -> IT Manager -> VP IT -> first CTO in the organization's 45+ year history.

Founded and ran a web development and IT consulting firm for 17 years -- full P&L ownership; delivered custom digital solutions for dozens of clients across content-driven and transactional verticals.
Architected and operated a proprietary commercial-grade CMS supporting dozens of production sites; direct client engagement across discovery, scope, architecture, build, and ongoing support.

projects.title: github.com/mknnmknn/about-mknn
projects.desc: Overview of public projects, with access to private repositories on request. Featured project: career-ops-dml -- a private fork of santifer/career-ops, customized into the LLM-assisted pipeline used daily in my job search. Working code extended alongside Claude Code (Node.js, Claude API, Playwright, Go).
